event_text: Utrecht receives city rights and grows as a religious and trading centre.
Den Bosch receives city rights in Brabant.
William II of Holland is killed during conflict with the West Frisians.
Amsterdam is first mentioned in a toll document.
The St Lucia flood causes major flooding and enlarges the Zuiderzee.
Count Floris V of Holland is murdered by nobles.
Holland is defeated by the Frisians at the Battle of Warns.
Burgundian influence in the Low Countries grows through dynastic marriage.
The St Elizabeth flood inundates parts of Holland and Zeeland.
Holland and Zeeland come under Burgundian rule.
Mary of Burgundy grants the Great Privilege to the provinces of the Low Countries.
Charles of Habsburg inherits the Burgundian Netherlands.
Charles becomes King of Spain, linking the Netherlands to the wider Habsburg empire.
The Seventeen Provinces are organised within the Burgundian Circle.
Philip II inherits the Netherlands from Charles V.
The Beeldenstorm iconoclastic riots spread through the Netherlands.
The Battle of Heiligerlee marks the opening of the Eighty Years War.
The Sea Beggars capture Brielle, boosting the Dutch Revolt.
The Union of Utrecht forms a defensive alliance of northern provinces.
The Act of Abjuration declares independence from Philip II.
The fall of Antwerp drives many merchants and artisans north.
The Dutch East India Company is founded.
The Twelve Years Truce begins between Spain and the Dutch Republic.
The Synod of Dort settles major Calvinist disputes.
The Dutch West India Company is founded.
Tulip mania collapses after speculative trading in tulip bulbs.
Rembrandt completes The Night Watch.
The Peace of Münster recognises Dutch independence.
The Battle of Dover opens the First Anglo-Dutch War over trade and naval power.
England declares war, beginning the Second Anglo-Dutch War.
The Rampjaar crisis brings invasion and political upheaval.
William III becomes king of England, Scotland and Ireland with Mary II.
William III dies and the second stadtholderless period begins.
The Treaty of Utrecht helps end the War of the Spanish Succession.
William IV becomes hereditary stadtholder of all provinces.
Britain declares war on the Dutch Republic, beginning the Fourth Anglo-Dutch War.
French-backed revolutionaries establish the Batavian Republic.
Napoleon creates the Kingdom of Holland.
The Netherlands is annexed into the French Empire.
The Kingdom of the Netherlands is created after the Congress of Vienna.
The Belgian Revolution separates Belgium from the Netherlands.
The Treaty of London recognises Belgian independence.
Constitutional reform makes the Netherlands a parliamentary democracy.
The Netherlands abolishes slavery in its colonies.
The North Sea Canal opens, improving access to Amsterdam.
Wilhelmina becomes queen as a child.
The Netherlands remains neutral during the First World War.
Constitutional reforms introduce universal male suffrage and school funding compromise.
Women gain the right to vote in the Netherlands.
The Afsluitdijk is completed, closing off the Zuiderzee.
Nazi Germany invades the Netherlands, beginning occupation during the Second World War.
Food shortages in the western Netherlands deepen into the Dutch famine during the final year of occupation.
The Netherlands is liberated from German occupation.
The Netherlands recognises Indonesian sovereignty.
The North Sea flood kills more than 1800 people in the Netherlands.
The Netherlands signs the Treaty of Rome as a founding EEC member.
Beatrix becomes queen after Juliana abdicates.
The Maastricht Treaty is signed in the Netherlands.
Euro banknotes and coins enter circulation in the Netherlands.
Willem-Alexander becomes king after Beatrix abdicates.
The COVID-19 pandemic affects the Netherlands.
